label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, let's read the passage and see what's going on. So, the main character is Mr. Williams, who is a careful driver. He comes to a crossroad with a "SLOW" sign. He slows down, looks both ways, and then drives across without stopping completely. Now, the question is about what he was doing when he was stopped by a policeman. The options are A through D. Let me break down the key points. First, the sign says "SLOW," not "STOP." So according to the rules mentioned earlier, when there's a "SLOW" sign, drivers should slow down but don't have to stop unless something is coming. Mr. Williams did slow down and looked both ways, then drove across without stopping. The policeman then stops him, saying he didn't stop at the crossing. Mr. Williams argues that the sign says "SLOW," and he did go slow. The policeman then realizes he's in the wrong street. So the question is asking what Mr. Williams was doing when he was stopped. Let's look at the options again: A. was driving at a high speed - but he did slow down, so this is probably not correct. B. was driving onto the main road - the passage says he came to the main road ahead, so maybe he was driving onto the main road when he was stopped. But when was he stopped? The policeman approached him after he drove across, so maybe he was already on the main road? Or was he in the process of driving onto it? C. was going to stop his car - but he didn't stop, he drove across. So C is not right. D. was driving slowly - yes, he did slow down as per the sign. But the question is about the moment he was stopped. The policeman blew the whistle
